overview: Eldrazi Tron aims to rapidly assemble Urza's Land Trio or use alternative acceleration methods to cast massive Eldrazi threats. The deck leverages mana acceleration and card manipulation to outpace opponents, while utilizing versatile tools like Karn, the Great Creator for sideboard flexibility. If the primary game plan stalls, it can still apply pressure through efficient mana development and targeted disruption.

keep_criteria: Hands that put your plan in motion straight away
Hands that accelerate your mana curve as much as possible
Hands that enable Ugin's Labyrinth on turn 1 and guarantee a Seer on turn 2
mulligan_criteria: Hands that give Karn on turn 4 with dead cards if green mana is inaccessible
Complicated hands that might fail to find the third Urza land piece
Hands with Chalice of the Void and Talismans when playing first against an unknown opponent

examples.reason: Enables Labyrinth on turn 1, may find another land with Devourer, guarantees a Seer on turn 2, and has Forge to speed up the plan.
May give Karn on turn 4 which is not ideal, and two cards will be dead if you can't access green mana.
Might not find the third piece needed, and Chalice and Talisman are not great if playing first against an unknown deck, potentially costing the game.

early_game: Complete Urza's Land Trio or accelerate mana curve through alternative means
Enable Ugin's Labyrinth on turn 1 to reduce Eldrazi costs
Use Devourer of Destiny to manipulate the top four cards and find lands or spells
Cast Mystic Forge to play spells directly from the library
mid_game: Play giant Eldrazi threats like Kozilek's Command or Thought-Knot Seer
Utilize Karn, the Great Creator to access sideboard hate cards
Apply pressure and survive early disruption while developing the board
late_game: Use Karn's +1 ability with Liquimetal Coating for land destruction
Cast Sowing Mycospawn to fix mana and destroy opponent's lands
Maintain board presence with resilient Eldrazi and targeted removal

key_cards.name: Ugin's Labyrinth
key_cards.notes: Goes really well with Eldratron's -cost cards and enables faster casting of Eldrazi.

key_cards.name: Devourer of Destiny
key_cards.notes: Needed in starting hand to look at top 4 cards, put one on top, and enable Labyrinth on the same turn.

key_cards.name: Mystic Forge
key_cards.notes: Lets you see the top card and play spells directly from the library to speed up the game plan.

key_cards.name: Karn, the Great Creator
key_cards.notes: Makes sideboard more versatile, allowing access to hate cards like Pithing Needle and Liquimetal Coating.

key_cards.name: Chalice of the Void
key_cards.notes: Excellent in main deck to disable opponent's main threats and artifacts, critical in many matchups.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize enabling Ugin's Labyrinth on turn 1 alongside Devourer of Destiny to manipulate the top cards and accelerate mana.
Use Mystic Forge early to bypass mana constraints and play spells directly from the library.
Deploy Kozilek's Command to develop the game plan and handle critical moments.
Avoid playing Chalice of the Void or Talismans on turn 1 if playing first against an unknown opponent, as they may cost the game.

matchups.advice: Survive the first few turns, pay attention to their hates, and follow your game plan. The matchup is favorable post-ban.
Use Chalice of the Void to disable their main threats. Struggle against disruption and creatures, so prioritize efficient mana acceleration.
Rely on Karn, the Great Creator and Chalice of the Void to disrupt their artifact synergy. They need a great opening hand to win on turn 2.
Guarantee a strong start to catch them off guard. Use Karn to get Pithing Needle and rely on graveyard hate in the sideboard.
Use Liquimetal Coating with Karn for land destruction. Thought-Knot Seer and Sowing Mycospawn are critical. Avoid messing with the main deck too much.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: the plan is to complete Urza's Land Trio as fast as possible to accelerate the mana curve and play giant Eldrazi on the board.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Devourer of Destiny is the card you need in your starting hand besides Labyrinth
Kozilek's Command is quite versatile. It develops your game plan
Thought-Knot Seer is extremely powerful, and Sowing Mycospawn is another way to use land destruction
Karn makes your sideboard more versatile, so removing pieces may make the deck less consistent

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Devourer of Destiny is the card you need in your starting hand besides Labyrinth... you can also enable Ugin's Labyrinth with it on that same turn
Mystic Forge is great at that. It lets you not only see the top card of your library but also play spells directly from there
Kozilek's Command is quite versatile. It develops your game plan and can be useful in many critical moments.
if you're playing first and don't know what your opponent is playing, Chalice of the Void and Talisman are not great, and may simply cost you the game.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: use Grinding Station to mill their entire deck with Underworld Breach in play, and then win with Thassa's Oracle.
Be careful with Psychic Frog, as it can easily derail the game
Boros was also hit by the latest bans, as it lost Amped Raptor
